title: coryphopteris petelotii
intro: Coryphopteris petelotii, also known as Vietnamese fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Coryphopteris petelotii is typically prepared for use in herbal medicine through infusions, decoctions, or topical applications, although its preparations and uses may vary depending on the region and cultural context.
